dialogue.utterance: Unit 1, you have a priority 2 call for breathing problems at a residence in Richmond.
Hi, I'm Lee, an EMT with the rescue squad. What's the main issue you'd like us to address today?
He's had abdominal pain for two days and feels really unwell now.
Can you clarify the main symptoms that caused you to call 911?
I've had cramping and pain starting on my right side. It's gotten much worse.
It's now spreading into my right shoulder. I've noticed a bit of discomfort.
Can you share your name and let us know where you are right now?
I'm John... I'm in Richmond. I thought it was just a stomach bug.
Could you tell me today's date for me?
Hmm, I think it's Tuesday? Maybe Wednesday. I'm not sure.
What brought things to the point where you called 911?
It's been painful - cramping in my stomach and pain on my right side for a couple days. It got worse today.
I'll check your eyes - this is part of our standard exam.
Can you tell me what comes after the number three?
His eyes open normally. Pupils are equal and reactive.
John is responding well to questions and following commands.
Airway is patent and not obstructed. No signs of swallowing issues.
Breathing is 18, even, and unlabored. No signs of cyanosis.
Pulse is strong and 114. Skin is warm, pale, and clammy with no cyanosis.
Blood pressure is 124 over 78. No signs of central or peripheral pulse issues.
Blood glucose is 0. No reported symptoms of low blood sugar.
Can we talk more about when your symptoms started?
The pain began about two days ago and has progressively gotten worse from there.
Did the pain come on suddenly or did it develop more gradually?
It started suddenly two days ago. It got a little better but came back stronger today.
Would you describe the pain as sharp, tugging, or heavy?
It's a cramping pain. It feels heavy sometimes too.
In your stomach, does it spread elsewhere when it gets worse?
It goes up to my right shoulder. Occasionally into my neck as well.
On a 0 to 10 scale, how bad is the pain for you right now?
I would say it's a 7. Sometimes worse when the cramps are strongest.
Is there anything that seems to trigger or ease the pain?
Sitting down helps, and eating sometimes helps too, but not consistently.
Are you noticing chills with it or other symptoms?
Yes, chills and cramps. I've also had some diarrhea.
If it changes, let me know so we can monitor and adjust care as needed.
Your palms and soles are slightly pale. Your skin feels cool and clammy to the touch.
John, can you tell me your name and the date again to check your mental status?
John is alert and oriented to time, place, and person.
He was answering our questions correctly. Showed potential signs of a CVA.
Pulse remains at 114. It's within a reasonable range for his condition.
Breathing is still at 18 and not labored. You're not in acute distress.
Blood pressure is currently 124 over 78, within expected norms.
Blood sugar remains at 0-no symptoms of low blood sugar reported.
Oxygen saturation is at 97%. Airway and breathing remain stable.
Partner, administer 324 mg of aspirin as per protocol now.
ASA has been given. No adverse reactions observed.
IV lock is in place and functioning-flushed and secured.
Let's attach the EKG now. Patient condition is stable for now.
12-lead EKG is being reviewed-shows right bundle branch block.
The EKG also shows signs of possible anterior ischemia.
Right axis deviation is present along with continuing sinus tachycardia.
Partner, we're following protocol 2-1 for suspected cardiac issues.
Pulse is slightly reduced to 109 and remains stable.
Blood pressure is now 112 over 72, a small but acceptable drop.
Oxygen saturation is currently 95%, still within acceptable range.
The aspirin is still effective with no reported adverse effects.
John, how is the pain at the moment? Has it lessened any?
It's a little better now. Maybe a 6, not as intense as before.
John, we're preparing to lay you down safely. We'll keep you comfortable.
Transport to Chippenham Hospital is appropriate. ED is aware.
John is now secured on the stretcher. All straps fastened and ready.
